targets.rationale: Raised to $5,400 end-2026 (Apr 2026). Structural central bank de-dollarization, tariff shock flight-to-safety, Fed pause. Liberation Day II tariffs add +$200 to gold premium.
Raised to $6,300 post-Liberation Day II. Trade war uncertainty, DXY weakness, EM central bank diversification away from USD. Most bullish major bank.
Bull-case $5,700 on tariff escalation + Fed pivot. Real rate decline and USD weakness post-tariff announcement key drivers.
Hartnett $6,000 target driven by dollar debasement, fiscal excess, and global safe-haven rotation. Tariff shock accelerates timeline.
Structural bull market intact. Physical demand + ETF inflows + CB buying trifecta. Tariff uncertainty adds safe-haven premium.
Raised target to $6,200. USD structural weakness, global demand diversification, tariff-driven safe-haven premium.
Upgraded to $6,000 — trade war systemic risk and Fed policy pivot. Dollar vulnerability a key tailwind.
Commodity supercycle in acceleration phase post-tariff shock. Gold preferred hard asset; $6,300 bull case on full trade war escalation.
Most bullish of the group. Persistent CB buying, tariff shock driving de-dollarization, gold breaking out of decade-long range.

data_quality.source: hardcoded published analyst targets (Q1-Q2 2026, post-Liberation Day II revisions)
data_quality.notes: Targets from major bank research notes (2025-2026). Updated when banks revise publicly. News snippets from Google News RSS.
